steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The hypothesis is that field of vision and sensitivity to alarm calls are inversely related: birds foraging in dense vegetation (narrow field of vision) should be more sensitive to alarm calls; birds foraging in open areas (wide field of vision) should be less sensitive. M. barbatus forages in dense vegetation — it should react. P. saturatus forages in open areas — it should not react.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — When Martinez and colleagues played T. caesius alarm calls, M. barbatus and P. saturatus displayed no reaction, whereas T. caesius displayed predator-avoidance behavior.: This shows M. barbatus not reacting, which contradicts the hypothesis (M. barbatus should react). T. caesius is the alarm-caller, not the species being tested. ✗
  • Choice B — Many local bird species with similar foraging habits to those of M. barbatus displayed no reaction when Martinez and colleagues played T. caesius alarm calls, whereas M. barbatus displayed predator-avoidance behavior.: M. barbatus reacting is consistent, but the comparison species are unrelated to the hypothesis about P. saturatus vs. M. barbatus. This does not test the predicted contrast between the two focal species. ✗
  • Choice C — Some individuals of P. saturatus displayed predator-avoidance behavior when Martinez and colleagues played T. caesius alarm calls, whereas nearly all did when M. barbatus alarm calls were played.: The hypothesis is about sensitivity to T. caesius alarm calls specifically, not M. barbatus calls. This choice introduces an irrelevant variable and shows P. saturatus reacting, which contradicts the prediction. ✗
  • Choice D — P. saturatus displayed no reaction when Martinez and colleagues played T. caesius alarm calls, whereas M. barbatus displayed predator avoidance behavior in response to the calls.: P. saturatus (open forager, wide vision) does not react — as predicted. M. barbatus (dense forager, narrow vision) reacts — as predicted. Both predictions confirmed simultaneously. ✓
That is the correct answer.
